title: Recon-PK series droid

The '''Recon-PK series droids''', colloquially known as '''rolo-droids''' or '''R-PKs''', were an [[Surveillance droid/Legends|intelligence-gathering droid]] series developed by [[Naftrat Odarr]] of [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]]. A modified version of the standard [[PK-series worker droid/Legends|PK-series worker droid]], the Recon-PK had its locomotion altered, replacing the original's bipedal format with a single [[wheel]]. Also modified was the makeup of the [[Droid/Legends|droid's]] cranial unit, which was packed full of audio/visual [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] equipment.

The Recon-PK soon became popular amongst operatives and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]]. Eventually, a batch of the droids was shipped to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where they were used by the various [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lords]] residing there. The Hutts used them to monitor their [[Slavery/Legends|slaves]], helping to prevent any attempts at escape. The tables were, in [[Time/Legends|time]], turned on the Hutts, when the young slave [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] began reprogramming the droids, causing them to turn a blind [[Eye/Legends|eye]] to any new escape attempts.

==Functions and capabilities==
With a cranial unit full of audio/visual sensors, the Recon-PK was highly adept at reconnaissance tasks. The droids were durable, so much so that they could withstand repeated exposure to the harsh conditions of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. As opposed to their predecessors, the bipedal [[PK-series worker droid/Legends|PK-series worker droid]], they featured a single-wheel locomotion. The droid's programming, though effective, was relatively simple, able to be reprogrammed by a child.<ref name="db" />

==History==
The financial success of the PK-series worker droid prompted its manufacturer, [[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]], to further the brand. In doing so, they employed former [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]] employee [[Naftrat Odarr]], a research & design officer. Odarr, using the original PK as a starting point, began to modify the unit for reconnaissance purposes. The new unit was released, and through word-of-mouth, became popular amongst mercenaries, company executives,<ref name="db" /> and [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]].<ref name="cswe">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref>

[[File:RoloLooksAtAnakin-TPM.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A Recon-PK looks at Anakin Skywalker.]]
Several units, through an under-the-table deal with the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]], found work on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], serving slave dealers and owners in the spaceport of [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]]. The slavers then used the small droids to monitor their property. The Recon-PKs did their job well, until one slave, six-year-old [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], found one of the units stuck beneath a reclining [[Dewback/Legends|dewback]]. Skywalker took the waylaid unit back to his residence to tinker with, and he was able to reprogram it. When he discovered its objective of spying on the slaves, the slave boy began to collect as many of the Recon-PKs as he could, and reprogrammed them, so that they would fail to report any pertient information on the actions of the slaves to their masters. As a result of Skywalker's modifications, seventeen slaves were able to escape Mos Espa over the years, until the boy was taken from Tatooine in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]].<ref name="db" /><ref name="tpm">{{Film|I}}</ref>

==Recon-PK series droids in the galaxy==
Apart from seeing widespread use on Tatooine, at least one Recon-PK was present at a [[Circus Horrificus/Legends|Circus Horrificus]] show on [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]] in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. The droid was shown on [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]], fleeing from an escaped [[arqet]].<ref name="hnn">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|52|regional/13418_3.html|Circus Horrificus Turns Horrific}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The "rolo-droids" first appeared, briefly, in [[George Lucas]]' [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']].<ref name="tpm" /> The design for the droids first came about simply because Lucas wanted a useless droid that would roll about on one wheel.<ref name="tpmart">''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace]]''</ref> It was later given a formal name and a canonical background by [[Neil Baker]] (under the alias "nob01")<ref name="Baker-Blog">{{Blog|bubbles|90|Roll up! Roll up! Get 'em while they're hot!}}</ref> through [[StarWars.com]]'s [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] feature, "[[What's The Story?]]"<ref name="DB update">{{SWArchive|url=databank/updates/news20070112.html|text=What's the Story: Episode I}}</ref>
